name: dumbbell alternate preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on the pad of the preacher bench, allowing your arms to fully extend.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand as you contract your biceps.
Continue to curl the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up. Rest your upper arms on the pad of the preacher bench, allowing your arms to fully extend. Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand as you contract your biceps. Continue to curl the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level. Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position. Repeat the movement with your left arm. Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
